features.name: Organized project architecture
capabilities: Project verification script checks Python source/script compilation, frontend JavaScript syntax, CLI help, and offline smoke regressions
Offline smoke tests cover auth redirect safety, password hashing, duplicate-user race handling, signal-review records, signal-review stats, revision enforcement, and deployment release contracts
Offline smoke tests exercise real Handler.do_POST routing for same-origin login blocking, authenticated preference saves, and manual signal-review evaluation triggers
Malformed JSON POST bodies, invalid UTF-8 request bodies, and invalid Content-Length headers return controlled 400 responses instead of generic server failures
Market, signal-review, and realtime symbol requests share normalization, deduplication, and an eight-symbol cap in offline regression coverage
Signal-review list requests validate the record limit as a bounded 1-1000 integer and return controlled 400 client errors
Offline smoke tests suppress expected error-path logs so verification output stays focused on real failures
PowerShell verification fails on native Python/Node command failures instead of continuing after a non-zero exit
Git ignore rules exclude local tool share packages, Python tool caches, and runtime lock/cache artifacts from accidental review
Restored scripts/smoke.py, scripts/deploy.py, .env.example, and docs/storage.md so verification and deployment documentation reference real files
Frontend offline smoke tests execute the production preference, timestamp, realtime freshness, bias, and symbol-limit functions in a sandboxed browser context
Frontend smoke coverage includes stale-revision retry, temporary storage retry, upstream-error badge state, and eight-custom-symbol restore after removing defaults

features.name: Strategy trust calibration and extreme-risk gates
capabilities: Historical 5m/15m/1h signal backtest metrics
Execution confidence applies trigger and risk penalties; the non-isomorphic 5m proxy backtest is informational only
Realtime-prejudge versus closed-candle confirmation labels
Extreme ATR/BOLL risk gates for small volatile coins
Entry trigger confirmation using 1m volume, structure, spread, and entry distance
Tick-size legal stop hints and grid stops
Separate direction quality score from executable opening score
Global no-open gate propagation across all timeframe cards
ATR-based near-entry trigger threshold plus 1m retest confirmation
Stop-first historical backtest path accounting with estimated fee/slippage cost
Backtest stop-out count, stop rate, average loss, net expectancy, and estimated cost fields
Funding-rate crowding risk gate downgrade
Order-book depth top5/top20 and depth imbalance in trigger confirmation
Unclosed 1m trigger candles forced to watch instead of confirmed
Strong frontend warnings for no-open gates, half-position bans, realtime-prejudge, and position conflicts
Shared online/backtest side threshold for signal selection
Backtest filtered-out sample count and ATR/volume filter note
Gross expectancy versus net expectancy after estimated fee/slippage
Price-path drawdown kept separate from execution cost
Reusable runtime backtest cache for analyzer/server calls
Realtime SSE depth20 stream with depth imbalance and top5/top20 depth
Realtime WebSocket hubs normalize symbol-set keys so differently cased shorthand symbols and different request orders share one backend stream
Realtime SSE requests for a symbol subset can reuse an already-active superset WebSocket hub to reduce duplicate Binance connections
Realtime diagnostics count exact/new/superset SSE sharing modes and expose reuse rate for multi-user connection monitoring

Realtime WebSocket workers are supervised from the SSE loop, restart after worker exit or stale upstream messages, expose restart_count diagnostics, and use generation tokens so stopped workers cannot overwrite replacement state
Realtime hub selection acquires clients under the registry lock so idle cleanup cannot orphan a newly reused hub
API error classification for invalid symbols, rate limits, timeouts, network errors, and upstream failures
Market API analyzer detail/stderr fields are hidden by default and only exposed when BIAN_EXPOSE_ERROR_DETAILS=1 for debugging
Partial-symbol API responses are rejected with missing/returned symbol metadata instead of cached as successful snapshots
Market cache keys treat symbols as a canonical set, so request order cannot duplicate analyzer work or Binance REST calls
Market and realtime endpoints reject more than eight unique symbols instead of silently truncating the request; the frontend enforces the same limit
Backtest cache writes use lock-file protection and unique temp files for concurrent analyzer subprocesses
Analyzer caches Binance exchangeInfo metadata per process so multi-symbol refreshes reuse tick-size and precision data instead of refetching it for every symbol
TLM extreme-rally downgrade is applied before side, backtest, and stop selection, with downgraded direction-score caps
Backend risk-budget sizing with suggested_size_pct, stop_distance_pct, max_loss_pct, and allowed/note metadata
Frontend account-risk fuse for daily loss, consecutive losses, and max single loss, blocking new-open suggestions until next local day

Opening-risk decision panel that automatically summarizes allow/wait/block status from strategy risk, trigger confirmation, candle state, stop validity, realtime price freshness, and manual fuse state

Opening guard distinguishes hard no-open blocks from wait states: trigger-confirmation failure and zero risk size are shown as waiting/failed-trigger guidance unless a true backend/account/invalid-price block exists
Frontend position display uses risk-budget percentage instead of score-to-star position labels
Same-side altcoin correlation exposure warning across loaded symbols
Frontend stale/warning/error_type handling for delayed cached snapshots
Realtime depth fields are preserved across bookTicker-only updates and strategy refreshes
Realtime depth20 snapshots refresh the displayed book midpoint and carry separate price/depth event timestamps so an old price cannot be kept artificially fresh

LIVE badge requires an open SSE transport, Binance upstream connected=true, and an active-symbol price newer than 20 seconds; stale heartbeat prices cannot toggle it green, and an explicit upstream error without fresh data renders offline
Browser LIVE freshness is based on local receipt time for a new server event identity, avoiding clock-skew errors without letting duplicate SSE heartbeats renew stale prices
Realtime reconnect state is shown separately from offline, with a one-second EventSource retry hint and configurable six-hour SSE response lifetime
Admin diagnostics expose realtime connect/disconnect counts and last connection, message, price, and depth timestamps for each shared hub
Realtime trigger overlay rechecks executable bid/ask distance, spread, depth freshness, top5 size, and imbalance without upgrading an old unconfirmed 1m structure
Confirmed realtime triggers expire after 90 seconds; strategy snapshots warn after 7 minutes and block new entries after 12 minutes
Missing or malformed strategy timestamps fail closed as unknown-age snapshots instead of being replaced with the browser's current time
Market cache TTL and live-review evaluation start at analysis completion/publication instead of before the analyzer subprocess runs
Funding countdown uses Binance nextFundingTime with mark/index price fields retained in the strategy report
Realtime entry, stop, chase, and strategy-snapshot drift warnings use ATR-adaptive thresholds instead of fixed percentages
Optional browser-local ALERT toggle plays cooldown-limited sound cues for visible near-entry and near-stop signal warnings
verification: python -B -m py_compile src/bian_dashboard/analyzer.py src/bian_dashboard/server.py bian.py server.py
GET /api/market?symbols=DOGEUSDT,TLMUSDT includes risk_sizing for top signal and every timeframe advice
GET /api/realtime-prices?symbols=DOGEUSDT streams depth_imbalance and top5 depth fields
limitations: Historical backtest is still a lightweight rolling signal-quality check; it now estimates fees/slippage and stop-first path, but does not model funding windows, liquidation, partial fills, queue position, account equity, or portfolio exposure.
Backtest samples are filtered by ATR/volume and should not be interpreted as all-market win rate.
The lightweight realtime trigger overlay can downgrade stale or weak live conditions, but new completed 1m volume/structure indicators are recalculated only on the next strategy snapshot.
Signals are suitable for assisted monitoring, not autonomous high-confidence trade execution.

features.name: TradingView Binance futures K-line panel
capabilities: Embedded TradingView advanced chart panel in the dashboard
Uses Binance USDT perpetual TradingView symbols such as BINANCE:DOGEUSDT.P
Follows the active dashboard symbol on tab switch, add, remove, and refresh
Provides 1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 4h, and 1D interval controls with local preference persistence
Provides direct external links to TradingView and Binance futures pages
limitations: The embedded chart depends on TradingView's external widget CDN being reachable from the browser.
The strategy calculations remain based on local Binance API data; the TradingView panel is a visual market-structure reference.

features.name: Optional MySQL and Redis storage
capabilities: Optional MySQL persistence for custom symbols, removed symbols, position state, account risk state, signal history, TradingView interval, and strategy snapshots, isolated by authenticated user
Per-user MySQL strategy snapshots retain a bounded recent history (1000 by default, configurable through BIAN_STRATEGY_SNAPSHOT_LIMIT) to prevent long-running storage growth
Optional Redis cache for short market payloads and latest realtime price/depth snapshots
Fresh runtime disk market snapshots are reused within the normal cache TTL after server restart before spawning a new analyzer subprocess
New GET/POST /api/preferences endpoints for browser-to-server preference sync
Preference POSTs require a positive per-user monotonic revision locked in the same MySQL transaction, and late requests with lower revisions are rejected before values are changed

Browser preference writes are serialized, reconcile rejected patches against current server state, retry only fields unchanged since the request snapshot, retry temporary saved=false storage responses, and reuse the original revision for in-flight-only sendBeacon/pagehide replay
Configured MySQL preference outages return HTTP 503 and remain on browser localStorage without treating the unavailable database as an empty revision-zero preference set
Redis read failures become cache misses with a 30-second cooldown, allowing memory, disk, or fresh analysis fallback
MySQL and Redis connection/read/write waits are bounded by documented environment settings, and availability health caches expire after five seconds
New GET /api/storage-status endpoint for checking configured storage availability
Browser localStorage remains the fallback when MySQL or Redis is not configured
verification: python -B -m py_compile src/bian_dashboard/storage.py src/bian_dashboard/server.py bian.py server.py
offline fake-connection coverage for preference revision ordering, Redis read degradation, MySQL timeout wiring, and health-cache expiry
limitations: MySQL and Redis require optional Python drivers and external services to be installed/configured.
The dashboard does not store full historical tick/order-book streams in MySQL; Redis stores only latest realtime snapshots.

features.name: Live signal review loop
capabilities: Persistent records for every directional timeframe advice after successful strategy snapshots
MySQL table bian_signal_reviews plus runtime/signal_reviews.json fallback when MySQL is unavailable, both isolated by authenticated user scope
Background signal-review evaluation loads due records across authenticated users and writes each result back to its owning user
Authenticated viewers receiving a fresh shared market cache still receive their own deduplicated live-review records without rerunning market analysis
Background 5m, 15m, and 1h evaluation using Binance USD-M 1m klines
Review snapshots use a publication-time book midpoint; stale analysis-start prices are not treated as publication prices
Review kline fetches start with the first complete 1m candle at or after publication and exclude candles that close after the exact horizon cutoff, preventing pre-publication high/low contamination
Waiting partial reviews are explicitly deferred and file fallback rows are fairly ordered, preventing old 15m/1h waits from starving newer due signals
Evaluation tracks entry reached, stop hit, same-bar stop ambiguity, max profit, max drawdown, final outcome, and failure reason
Live-review max profit and outcome are net of estimated round-trip fee/slippage, while gross fields are preserved for audit/export
Legacy live-review records without cost fields are normalized to net outcomes at API/statistics read time so old gross samples do not inflate hit rate
Background live-review evaluation triggers are debounced so repeated page/API refreshes do not spawn redundant evaluator threads
GET /api/signal-reviews returns recent records and aggregate stats for active symbols
Dashboard panel shows real sample count, triggered count, hit rate, stop rate, not-triggered rate, average max profit/drawdown/outcome, and recent outcomes
Dashboard review panel shows live-review evaluator status so users can distinguish started, cooling down, running, and storage-unavailable states
Live-review sample-quality labels prevent low-sample or incomplete-horizon stats from being read as reliable win rate
Live-review calibration is segmented by risk gate, trigger status, and candle state so weak signal buckets can be identified before changing thresholds
Invalid live-review signals such as illegal entry/stop are counted separately in failure stats and segment invalid rates instead of being hidden from summary quality
Live-review stats and UI surface due-pending evaluation backlog so missing samples are distinguishable from delayed background evaluation
Authenticated users can manually trigger a debounced live-review evaluation catch-up from the dashboard review panel
New live-review records persist structured market regime buckets from the advice anchor interval, including trend, ATR regime, BOLL width regime, and BOLL position regime
Dashboard can export the active symbol's live signal review records as UTF-8 CSV with horizon outcomes and regime fields
Deployment docs explain that CSV net fields should drive hit-rate/expectancy review while gross fields are for raw price-path audit

Live-review calibration candidates require sufficient independent signals and independent triggers, and use the declared 1h terminal outcome so overlapping 5m/15m/1h paths cannot inflate threshold-tuning confidence
verification: python -B -m py_compile src/bian_dashboard/storage.py src/bian_dashboard/server.py bian.py server.py
offline evaluate_horizon_from_klines smoke test for current-minute entry, exact horizon cutoff, and non-predating entry time
limitations: Evaluation uses 1m candle OHLC, so same-bar entry and stop order is treated conservatively as ambiguous/stop-first; a partial publication minute is excluded and can miss a real touch before the next full minute.

Net live-review outcomes subtract estimated fee/slippage but still do not model funding payments, liquidation, partial fills, order-book queue position, exchange outages, margin mode, or fast-market slippage expansion.
The loop records signal quality for calibration; it does not automatically retune strategy thresholds yet.
Fresh deployments need time to accumulate live samples before hit-rate statistics become meaningful.

features.name: Docker Compose deployment
capabilities: Container image for the Python dashboard API and static web server
Compose stack with isolated dashboard, MySQL, and Redis services
Named Docker volumes for runtime cache, MySQL data, and Redis append-only data
Configurable public port and storage credentials through environment variables
Container host binding through BIAN_HOST/BIAN_PORT
Docker health checks surface a fresh auth deployment that has no users and no first-admin secret instead of leaving an unusable login page
Python deployment script skips protected /api/market curl checks when auth is enabled and asks for logged-in verification instead

Python deployment script requires a clean Git worktree by default, preserves repository/global/Git-info ignore rules, fails closed when ignore configuration is unreadable, excludes local .env, and offers an explicit --allow-dirty development override

Remote deployment stages a fresh release directory, preserves the previous release and uploaded archive until health passes, persists --public-port in remote .env, and supports bounded SSH/SCP retries
Docker Compose passes an optional Redis password through the dashboard client, Redis requirepass, and authenticated healthcheck; fresh scripted deployments generate the secret
verification: python -B -m py_compile src/bian_dashboard/storage.py src/bian_dashboard/server.py bian.py server.py
server-side docker compose config passed under /opt/bian-dashboard
server-side docker compose up -d --build started healthy dashboard, MySQL, and Redis containers
GET http://159.223.91.36:8000/api/storage-status reports MySQL and Redis available
limitations: Server access to Binance APIs still depends on the hosting provider network not being blocked or rate-limited by Binance.
Production secrets should be set in a server-only .env file, not committed.

features.name: Database-backed login gate
capabilities: MySQL-backed dashboard users and sessions
PBKDF2-SHA256 password hashes instead of plaintext passwords
HttpOnly session cookie with hashed session token stored in MySQL
Server-side auth gate for dashboard pages, APIs, preferences, and realtime SSE
Public health endpoint for Docker health checks
Public /api/health returns non-secret deployment diagnostics including uptime, auth readiness, storage availability, cache counts, and required runtime path checks
/api/health returns HTTP 503 when auth is enabled but MySQL is unavailable, or when a fresh empty users table has no first-admin secret configured
Auth readiness reports has_users, login_ready, and issue fields without exposing bootstrap usernames or passwords
Admin-only /api/diagnostics returns operator cache, analyzer, storage, realtime WebSocket state, and signal-review queue state without exposing credentials

Dashboard header shows an admin-only diagnostics button with a readable diagnostics modal, realtime sharing counters, signal-review queue/evaluator counters, live-review cost assumptions, and a raw JSON fallback
Login and logout endpoints plus dashboard logout button
Authenticated password change endpoint and dashboard modal
Password changes verify the current password, store a fresh PBKDF2-SHA256 hash, and invalidate other sessions for the same user
Admin-only user registration endpoint and dashboard modal for creating user or admin accounts
Bootstrap admin creation only when the users table is empty
Basic per-IP login failure lockout
Sanitized login next redirects to prevent external or API-target redirects

Dashboard POST APIs, including login, enforce same-origin Origin/Referer host checks by default, reject present null or malformed source values, and preserve non-browser CLI compatibility only when no source header is present; X-Forwarded-Host is trusted only behind trusted local/private proxies
Unexpected 500 errors on login, password change, user creation, preferences, and signal-review APIs return a generic browser-safe message while details stay in server logs
Optional trusted-proxy X-Forwarded-For support through BIAN_AUTH_TRUST_PROXY_HEADERS, disabled by default
Session last_seen_at touch interval through BIAN_AUTH_SESSION_TOUCH_INTERVAL_SECONDS to reduce MySQL write load
Duplicate admin-created usernames return a controlled duplicate-user error even under concurrent requests
verification: python -B -m py_compile src/bian_dashboard/storage.py src/bian_dashboard/server.py scripts/deploy.py bian.py server.py
password-hash smoke test validates the right password and rejects a wrong password
auth username validation smoke test covers accepted and rejected account names
local auth smoke: health 200, dashboard redirects to /login, login page renders
safe login redirect smoke covers external URLs, protocol-relative URLs, API paths, and valid dashboard paths
offline smoke verifies /api/health payload shape, auth-readiness failure, and checks that password/bootstrap details are not exposed
offline smoke verifies /api/diagnostics payload shape and checks that password/bootstrap details are not exposed
limitations: Bootstrap credentials are still provided through server-only environment variables for first-user creation.
Anonymous public registration is intentionally not exposed; only admin users can create accounts.
There is not yet a web UI for disabling users.
Set BIAN_AUTH_COOKIE_SECURE=1 when serving behind HTTPS.
Set BIAN_AUTH_TRUST_PROXY_HEADERS=1 only behind a trusted local/private reverse proxy.
